title: Music School Management
persona: Music school administrators, teachers, and students
narrative: An admin sets up the term, teachers get assigned to classes, students enroll, lessons happen, and progress is tracked.

activities.title: Set up the term
activities.description: I create classes, schedule rooms, and assign teachers for the upcoming term

activities.title: Enroll students
activities.description: Students (or parents) browse classes and sign up

activities.title: Teach lessons
activities.description: Teachers take attendance and log what was covered

activities.title: Track progress
activities.description: Teachers and parents see how students are developing over time

activities.title: Bill and report
activities.description: Admin generates invoices and end-of-term reports

releases.label: MVP
releases.goal: A music school can run a full term digitally — from class creation to end-of-term reporting — replacing spreadsheets and paper.

releases.label: Progress tracking
releases.goal: Teachers and parents can see student development — progress is visible, not hidden in notebooks.

releases.label: Self-service and growth
releases.goal: The school can scale to more students without more admin overhead — enrollment, scheduling, and billing are automated.

stories.title: Waitlist with auto-promotion when spot opens
Automatic invoice generation at term end
